title: rubus vulgaris subsp. macrophyllus
intro: Rubus vulgaris subsp. macrophyllus, also known as the European giant raspberry or large-leaved bramble, is a subspecies of the bramble genus Rubus. This plant is commonly found in various parts of Europe and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The leaves and roots of Rubus vulgaris subsp. macrophyllus have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial for various health conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, and is also used as a food source, particularly its berries which are rich in vitamins and minerals.
